commit.message: Docs: align Marble release state with build 29

Docs: record 1.9 (build 29) VALID on TestFlight

Build 29 (the Personal Records feature) processed VALID on App Store Connect
(build id e61a527f-4780-4e10-9f95-fdf0914cb0ec) and is available to the
internal all-builds group "test group A". Landed on the first publish attempt.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Personal Records: celebrate and chase per-exercise PRs

Add an all-time personal-records system (weight & reps) to make beating
your best the core motivation loop.

Engine (marble/Components/PersonalRecords.swift, pure + unit-tested):
- badges(for:): walks each exercise's sets in performedAt order and badges
  every record-breaker (incl. the baseline first set) so the journal shows a
  trail of PRs and the all-time best is always badged.
- records(for:entries:): all-time heaviest (tie-break more reps) + most-reps
  (tie-break heavier), each as its full weight x reps combo, plus the usual
  working range over a recent window.
- projectedBadge(...): drives the live "New PR!" cue while logging (fires only
  vs an existing best). Weight comparisons unit-normalize lb<->kg.

UI:
- PRBadgeLabel: monochrome trophy pill, rendered on its own row line so it
  never squeezes the exercise name at large Dynamic Type (textClipped-safe).
- SetRowView + QuickLogCardView: show the badge on record sets.
- JournalView: PR badge map computed inside the existing RenderMemo derivation
  (JournalDerived{sections, prBadges}), captured once per body.
- AddSetView: "Personal best" target card (PersonalBestCardView) + live PR
  banner; MarbleHaptics.celebrate() on a record save.

Tests: PersonalRecordsTests (12) -> 164 unit; two new JournalFlowUITests for
the journal badge and the AddSet card + live banner. Accessibility audit green.

Bump to 1.9 (build 29) for TestFlight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: refresh test/build state — 152 unit + 28 UI + audit, scan+Vision tests, build 28

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Test: prove the real Vision OCR step extracts text (scan pipeline integration)

Renders clear printed workout text and runs it through VisionWorkoutTextRecognizer
(not a mock), asserting non-empty output containing the numbers. Verifies the
on-device OCR layer of the handwritten-scan feature actually works.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Verify scan flow + fix clobbered accessibility identifier

- ImportView: drop the VStack 'Import.Scan' container identifier that was
  overriding the 'Scan a Workout' button's own 'Import.Scan.Open' (SwiftUI
  propagates a container's accessibilityIdentifier onto its children). No
  user-facing change; restores the button's intended automation identifier.
- Add ScanFlowUITests: drives Journal -> Import hub -> Scan, asserts the capture
  screen renders and the photo picker is available, then returns to the hub.

Merge handwritten workout scan feature
Merge perf + iOS 26 modernization (memoized derivations, @Observable, supplement index)

Add handwritten workout scan (on-device OCR + parse to journal import)

- Vision OCR (VNDocumentCameraViewController + VNRecognizeText), deterministic
  HandwrittenWorkoutParser, optional FoundationModels parser (iOS 26+) with fallback
- WorkoutScanView flow (capture -> process -> review -> import); reuses
  WorkoutImportMapper.resolveExercise + ImportedWorkout dedup ledger
- ImportView: add Scan a Workout section; ImportedWorkout: add .photoScan source
- NSCameraUsageDescription added (Debug + Release)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Perf + iOS 26: memoize Trends/Calendar/Journal derivations, @Observable, index supplements

- RenderMemo: cache view-derived data keyed by a cheap Equatable signature
- Trends/Calendar/Journal: stop re-deriving (filter/group/sort) in body on every
  render incl. chart scrubbing; fold PR-card bests + accessibility strings into
  the memoized TrendsDerivedData
- @Observable migration: QuickLogCoordinator, TabSelection, ImportViewModel
- #Index on SupplementEntry.takenAt (mirrors SetEntry.performedAt)
- Tests: RenderMemoTests, TrendsDerivedDataTests (115 unit tests green)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Ship Marble 1.9 build 26
Ship Marble 1.9 build 25
Fix calendar month layout overlap
Ship Marble 1.9 build 24 resilience pass

Rest timer: Live Activity foundation + interactive-log integration

Adds the between-sets rest-timer Live Activity, wired end-to-end except the
widget-extension target (which must be added in Xcode — see MarbleWidgets/SETUP.md):

- RestTimerAttributes: shared ActivityAttributes (nonisolated for Sendable under
  the project's MainActor default isolation).
- RestActivityController: starts/replaces/ends a single rest timer; safe no-op when
  rest is 0, during UI tests, or when Live Activities are unavailable/disabled — so
  this is harmless before the extension exists. Pure start/timing logic unit-tested.
- Integration: fires after each INTERACTIVE save (AddSet, Journal 'Log Again' +
  duplicate, Set-detail duplicate); bulk import intentionally does not trigger it.
- MarbleWidgets/: the extension's Live Activity UI (Lock Screen + Dynamic Island) and
  WidgetBundle, plus SETUP.md with the exact target-wiring steps.

103 unit tests green (app target). MarbleWidgets/ compiles once added to a target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Persistence: test store self-recovery via an injectable store URL

Generalize PersistenceController's recovery flow into makeRecoveringContainer(at:)
(plus URL-parameterized makePersistentContainer/backupCorruptStore/sidecarURLs) so
production behaviour is unchanged but tests can drive it against a throwaway store
in a temp directory.

PersistenceRecoveryTests covers: (1) on-disk write -> reopen -> read round-trip;
(2) a corrupt (non-SQLite) store is moved aside to *.corrupt and replaced with a
fresh, usable store instead of crashing. Confirms ModelContainer init actually
throws on a bad store and the documented recovery path runs (101 unit tests green).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: record recommended 1.9 Strava posture (ship unconfigured)

Ship 1.9 with Strava keys absent so only the verified Apple Health +
Garmin-via-Health paths go out; promote Strava in 1.10 after a live OAuth
pass and a decision on the in-binary client_secret. Strava is hidden unless
keys are configured, so this is the default — no code change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: refresh test count (99) and record unpushed import polish

- TESTING.md / RELEASE_HANDOFF.md: unit suite is now 99 tests (was 94).
- RELEASE_HANDOFF.md: note local release/1.9 is 1 commit ahead of origin
  with import polish (HealthKit average HR, env-configurable Strava,
  import-hub test coverage); add ImportFlowUITests + HR to the verified /
  needs-a-live-pass lists. Date bumped to 2026-06-20. No version/build change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Import: HealthKit heart rate, env-configurable Strava, and import-hub test coverage

- HealthKit: read average heart rate per workout via a discrete-average
  HKStatisticsQuery over each workout's time window (covers Apple Watch and
  bridged sources like Garmin/Wahoo), so imported notes show "NNN bpm avg".
  Adds .heartRate to the requested read types.
- Strava: StravaConfiguration.resolve(infoDictionary:environment:) now reads
  credentials from environment variables (STRAVA_CLIENT_ID/_SECRET/
  _REDIRECT_URI/_SCOPE) first, then Info.plist. Makes "Strava if keys are
  configured" usable for local dev without committing secrets; release builds
  keep the Info.plist path. Behaviour is unit-tested and dependency-injected.
- Tests: 5 unit tests for Strava credential resolution; an ImportFlowUITests
  flow that opens the import hub from the Journal and verifies Apple Health +
  the Garmin-via-Health bridge render and dismiss.
- Docs: INTEGRATIONS.md documents the two-path Strava setup and the real
  HealthKit average-HR behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Docs: document the workout import hub and refresh project docs

- Add INTEGRATIONS.md: how each import source works and why (ToS-aligned, no
  backend; Apple Health bridge, Garmin via Health, Strava OAuth + setup steps).
- README: refreshed overview, architecture, and a documentation map.
- AGENTS: updated import folder layout and a "no reverse-engineered logins" rule.
- RELEASE_HANDOFF: import-hub summary, 94-test count, build-20 consistency note.
- TESTING / AdditionalDocumentation: minor accuracy fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace Garmin login with a ToS-aligned workout import hub

Marble now imports workouts as a UI layer over multiple services, using only
officially sanctioned, backend-free paths:

- Apple Health is the universal bridge; each workout is labeled by its true
  origin (Garmin, Strava, Apple Watch, ...) from HealthKit source/device metadata.
- Garmin comes in through Apple Health (Garmin Connect -> Apple Health). The
  earlier unofficial on-device Garmin login is removed -- it reverse-engineered
  Garmin's private flow and violated their Terms of Service.
- Strava is a direct, official OAuth 2.0 connector (ASWebAuthenticationSession,
  Keychain tokens with auto-refresh, athlete/activities summaries). It stays
  hidden until its Info.plist credentials are configured.

Also adds NSHealthUpdateUsageDescription. 94 unit tests green on Xcode 26.5 /
iOS 26.2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Bump version to 1.9 (build 20) for TestFlight

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden data safety, import feature, and add CI

Data safety:
- Add explicit SwiftData VersionedSchema + MarbleMigrationPlan (MarbleSchema.swift).
- ModelContainer recovers from a failed migration (backs up *.corrupt, recreates,
  in-memory fallback) instead of fatal-crashing on launch; drop force-unwraps.
- Gate the default split plan behind a one-time flag so deleting it sticks.

Import feature:
- Add a DB-level unique deduplicationKey to ImportedWorkout; importer queries by it.
- HealthKit: remove force-unwraps on quantity types.
- Garmin: skip activities with no stable id/date instead of fabricating "0"/now;
  resolve credentials from Info.plist and hide the source until configured.
- Surface import failures in the UI instead of only a haptic.
- Fix category inference (Leg Curl -> hamstrings).

Swift 6 readiness:
- Mark SwiftData-serialized value types nonisolated under MainActor default isolation
  (ExerciseMetricsProfile, MetricRequirement, ExerciseMetricKind, DistanceUnit,
  formattedDuration, KeychainTokenStore, ImportOutcome). App build is warning-clean.

Privacy + CI:
- Add PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy (no tracking/collection; UserDefaults + file-timestamp reasons).
- Add GitHub Actions workflow running the unit suite on PRs/release pushes.

Tests:
- Add ImportProviderMappingTests and ImportViewModelTests; extend mapper tests.
- All 89 unit tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add HealthKit + Garmin Connect workout import

- HealthKit (Apple Watch) provider with read-only workout/distance/energy auth
- Garmin Connect provider (OAuth via ASWebAuthenticationSession, keychain token)
- Importer with idempotent (source, externalID) dedupe ledger via ImportedWorkout
- Mapper resolves/creates exercises case-insensitively with per-batch cache
- Drop unused heartRate from HealthKit read request (privacy hygiene)
- Import sheet surfaced from Journal toolbar
Add progress media crop editing polish
Document release handoff for agents

Prepare Marble 1.8 build 19

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add run logging with distance presets, pace, and hour-long durations

Runs are now first-class:
- New Run exercise category (figure.run icon, run emoji set); quads moves
  to figure.step.training with a runtime fallback
- Run template in the create-exercise flow (distance + time, defaults to
  kilometers, auto-applies the Run category; Sprint stays meters-based)
- One-tap distance preset chips in the set logger, unit-aware: 100-1500 m,
  1/3/5/10/21.1/42.2 km, 1-26.2 mi, plus yard/foot sets
- Duration picker gains hours (0-12) and full 1-second precision, styled
  to the brand; durations format as "1h 15m" and "1:02:05" past the hour
- Pace is real runner pace now: "4:32 /km" or "7:18 /mi" derived from each
  entry's own unit, shown in Trends PRs and progress drill-downs
- Distance comparisons normalize to meters, so a 6 mi run correctly beats
  a 200 m sprint in Best Distance / Fastest Pace regardless of logged unit
- Quick-log card summary now includes distance; Run added to seed data
  and the screenshot fixture

Unit (61), snapshot (re-recorded + verified), accessibility audit, and the
full UI suite all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Declare export-compliance exemption in build settings

Embeds ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=NO in generated Info.plists so future
uploads carry the declaration instead of needing a post-upload API update.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prepare Marble 1.8 build 18

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign Trends charts with per-metric color accents

Trends is the one surface where the monochrome brand allows color; each
chart now has a single restrained accent identity, tuned brighter on dark:

- Consistency (indigo): smooth monotone line over a gradient area fill,
  plus a dashed average-per-active-bucket rule
- Weekly Volume (orange/amber/terracotta): gradient bars with rounded
  tops and a custom colored-dot legend replacing the default
- Supplements (teal): gradient area + line with dots on logged buckets
- Exercise Progress (violet): same area treatment with point emphasis
- Personal records: gold ring markers and a gold tooltip trophy
- Selection: accent halo dot (soft glow + background-ringed core) and
  accent rule instead of grey dash
- Summary cards: small accent identity dots tying cards to their charts

Snapshot baselines re-recorded for Trends and Exercise Progress. Unit,
snapshot, and accessibility audit suites pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add deterministic App Store screenshot tooling

- New screenshots fixture mode seeds five weeks of realistic push/pull/legs
  history with progression, Sunday recovery, and daily supplements
- MARBLE_INITIAL_TAB and MARBLE_OPEN_QUICK_LOG launch hooks drive each tab
  (and the set logger sheet) for repeatable captures

Used to produce the 1.8 App Store screenshots (APP_IPHONE_65 1284x2778 via
iPhone 14 Plus sim, APP_IPAD_PRO_3GEN_129 2064x2752 via iPad Pro 13 sim).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prepare Marble 1.8 build 17

The 1.7 train is closed in App Store Connect (upload rejected with
ITMS-90186/90062), and 1.8 is the active train with build 16 latest,
so this release joins it as build 17.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Apply Apple best-practice performance and UX overhaul

Performance:
- Compute Journal day sections, Calendar markers/streak/day summary, and all
  Trends aggregates in a single pass per render instead of chained computed
  properties that re-grouped the full entry list many times per frame
- Bucket Trends consistency/supplement charts weekly for 1Y/All ranges, with
  week-level tooltips and drill-down sheets
- Add SwiftData #Index on SetEntry.performedAt
- Defer first-launch seeding off the launch critical path (sync under UI tests)

UX / HIG alignment:
- Add App Intents + App Shortcuts (open quick log, log last set again)
- Let system Liquid Glass own nav/tab/sheet surfaces; custom backgrounds only
  under Reduce Transparency; remove dead pre-iOS 26 fallbacks; minimize tab
  bar on scroll
- Add haptic confirmations for set save, log-again, duplicate, delete, undo
- Re-anchor date-relative UI (Today/Yesterday, calendar header) on scene
  activation and significant time changes
- Scale fixed-size SF Symbol icons and emoji tiles with Dynamic Type; let
  quick-log card labels wrap instead of clipping at large type sizes
- Route system undo gestures through the SwiftData context; replace silent
  try? saves with rollback-on-failure and user-visible errors in Journal

Fixes:
- Duplicating or undo-restoring a set no longer drops distance/distanceUnit

Snapshot baselines re-recorded for the intentional visual changes (system
glass bars, Dynamic Type icon scaling). Unit, snapshot, UI, and accessibility
audit suites all pass. Build 5.
